text: The robotic system uses the left arm to grab the bottom door handle, the furthest right arm to grab the top door handle, and the middle arm to grasp the color-coded shapes.
The lateral arms are positioned to avoid collisions with the middle arm, facilitating optimal grabbing of color-coded shapes.
The robotic arms maneuver to grab the stem of the color-coded shapes with a vertical orientation, carefully placing them into panels with depth to avoid excessive force or collisions.
The surgeon coordinates the three robotic arms, continuously adjusting the camera and making small movements to prevent collisions and maintain all elements within the field of view.
The robotic arms place a blue piece into its designated receptacle, then grab a green shape to return it to its original receptacle on the far right.
The surgeon swaps the middle and right robotic arms multiple times, adjusting the camera to achieve an optimal position for safely placing the green piece back into its designated receptacle.
